hypothesis: If open-weight language models fine-tuned on BCC/B2 compositions are further optimized with Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical preferences, then they will generate a higher fraction of valid, novel BCC/B2 alloy pairs that satisfy phase coexistence, correct formation order, room-temperature B2 stability, and <10% impurity with lower lattice mismatch than SFT-only and non-physics baselines, because physics-grounded preference learning steers the generative distribution toward thermodynamically feasible regions while limiting distribution shift.
